title: Introduction
description: Official Talonic MCP server for AI agents. Seven tools and one resource for structured document extraction via the Model Context Protocol.

content.text: The `@talonic/mcp` package is the official Talonic MCP server. It gives AI agents seven tools for extracting structured, schema-validated data from any document via the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io).

When an agent needs to pull structured data out of a PDF, scan, image, or messy document, the usual approach is raw OCR plus an LLM call. Results are unreliable; tables get mangled, dates get misread, totals drift.

With this MCP server installed, the agent has a `talonic_extract` tool that returns schema-validated JSON with per-field confidence scores, a detected document type, and stable IDs for follow-up calls.
Listed on the [official MCP Registry](https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/) as `io.github.talonicdev/talonic-mcp`.

faq.question: What is the Talonic MCP server?
faq.answer: An official Model Context Protocol server that gives AI agents seven tools for document extraction, search, filtering, and schema management via the Talonic API.

title: Installation
seoTitle: Install Talonic MCP Server — Talonic Docs
description: Install the Talonic MCP server with a one-line npx invocation. No clone, no build required.

content.text: The package is on npm. Every MCP client launches it the same way: a one-line `npx` invocation with your API key in the `env` block. No clone, no build.
The `-y` flag skips the npm install prompt. Pinning to `@latest` means new versions are picked up on the next client restart. To pin to a specific version, replace `@latest` with e.g. `@0.1.6`.

faq.question: How do I install the Talonic MCP server?
faq.answer: Add a one-line npx invocation to your MCP client config. No clone or build required. The -y flag skips prompts and @latest picks up new versions on restart.

title: Authentication
description: Get a Talonic API key for the MCP server. Each workspace is isolated with private documents and schemas.

content.text: Each user runs against their own isolated Talonic workspace. Your documents and schemas are private to you.

items: Sign up at [https://app.talonic.com](https://app.talonic.com). Free tier: 50 extractions per day, no credit card.
Settings → API Keys → Create New Key.
Copy the `tlnc_` value into your MCP client config (see client setup pages).

title: Claude Desktop
seoTitle: MCP Setup for Claude Desktop — Talonic Docs
description: Step-by-step setup for the Talonic MCP server in Claude Desktop on macOS and Windows.

content.text: Edit `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS) or `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` (Windows):
Fully restart Claude Desktop (Cmd+Q on macOS, not just close the window). Talonic appears in the connected servers list with all seven tools.

seoTitle: MCP Setup for Cursor — Talonic Docs
description: Configure the Talonic MCP server in Cursor IDE.

content.text: Edit `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (or open Cursor settings → MCP → edit config):

title: Cline
seoTitle: MCP Setup for Cline — Talonic Docs
description: Configure the Talonic MCP server in the Cline VS Code extension.

content.text: Open the Cline panel → settings (gear icon) → MCP Servers → Edit. Add the standard config entry and save. Restart the panel.

title: Continue
seoTitle: MCP Setup for Continue — Talonic Docs
description: Configure the Talonic MCP server in Continue for VS Code and JetBrains.

content.text: Edit `~/.continue/config.json`. Add to the `mcpServers` array:

title: Cowork
seoTitle: MCP Setup for Cowork — Talonic Docs
description: Configure the Talonic MCP server in Cowork.

content.text: Open Cowork settings → MCP Servers → Add. Use the same shape as Claude Desktop above.

title: talonic_extract
description: Extract structured, schema-validated data from a document. Accepts file_data, file_path, file_url, or document_id with a schema or schema_id.

content.text: Extract structured, schema-validated data from a document. Returns clean JSON with per-field confidence scores.

params.name: file_data
params.description: Base64-encoded file bytes. Recommended for chat clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor).

params.name: filename
params.description: Original filename. Required with `file_data` for MIME type inference.

params.name: file_path
params.description: Local filesystem path to the document.

params.name: file_url
params.description: Public URL to fetch the document from.

params.name: document_id
params.description: ID of an already-uploaded document in the workspace.

params.name: schema
params.description: Inline schema definition (JSON Schema or flat key-type map).

params.name: schema_id
params.description: ID of a saved schema in the workspace (UUID form).

content.text: Always provide either a `schema` or `schema_id`. Auto-discovery (no schema) currently returns 500 on production.

title: talonic_search
description: Omnisearch across documents, fields, sources, and schemas in the Talonic workspace.

content.text: Omnisearch across documents, extracted field values, field names, sources, and schemas in the workspace. Use for conceptual or fuzzy queries.

title: talonic_filter
description: Filter documents by extracted field values using composable conditions: eq, gt, between, contains, and more.

content.text: Filter documents by extracted field values using composable conditions (`eq`, `gt`, `between`, `contains`, etc.). Accepts canonical field names (e.g. `vendor.name`, `policy.0_coverage_type`) which the Talonic API resolves to IDs server-side, or UUIDs directly.
The `is_not_empty` operator currently underreports. Use a more specific operator against a known value when possible.

title: talonic_get_document
description: Fetch full metadata for a single document by ID, including processing log and link URLs.

content.text: Fetch full metadata for a single document by ID, including processing log and link URLs.

title: talonic_to_markdown
description: Get OCR-converted markdown for a document. Accepts document_id, file_data, file_path, or file_url.

content.text: Get OCR-converted markdown for a document. Accepts `document_id` (cheapest — no re-processing), `file_data` + `filename`, `file_path`, or `file_url`.

title: talonic_list_schemas
description: List all saved schemas with their definitions from the Talonic workspace.

content.text: List all saved schemas with their definitions. Use before `talonic_extract` to find an existing `schema_id` instead of defining an inline schema.

title: talonic_save_schema
description: Save a schema definition to the workspace for reuse across extractions.

content.text: Save a schema definition to the workspace for reuse. Returns the saved schema with its ID.
Prefer full JSON Schema format for now. The flat key-type map may return a "no fields" error on the current server version.

title: talonic://schemas
description: The MCP resource URI that exposes saved schemas to clients that browse resources separately, like Claude Desktop and Cowork.

content.text: The `talonic://schemas` resource exposes the saved-schemas list to clients that browse MCP resources separately. Claude Desktop and Cowork render these in the UI.

title: Drag & Drop in Chat
seoTitle: MCP Drag and Drop File Handling — Talonic Docs
description: How file_data + filename solves the sandboxed file path problem for drag-and-drop in Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Cowork.

content.text: When a user drag-drops a file into a chat-style MCP host (Claude Desktop, Cowork, Cursor), the file lands in a host-owned sandbox directory the MCP server cannot read. The path the host hands the agent is meaningless to a separately-running `npx` MCP process.

From `@talonic/mcp@0.1.4`, agents can pass **`file_data`** (base64-encoded file bytes) and **`filename`** on `talonic_extract` and `talonic_to_markdown`. The agent reads the file bytes from the conversation, base64-encodes them, and passes them through the MCP tool call. The MCP server decodes, infers MIME type from the filename, and uploads to the Talonic API as a normal multipart request.
Tool descriptions advertise `file_data` as the recommended input for chat-style clients, so well-trained agents reach for it automatically. No client-side configuration required.

faq.question: How does file upload work in MCP chat clients?
faq.answer: From v0.1.4, agents pass file_data (base64) + filename instead of a file path. This handles drag-and-drop in sandboxed chat clients like Claude Desktop.

title: Architecture
description: How the Talonic MCP server connects agents to the Talonic API via the Model Context Protocol over stdio.

content.text: The MCP server sits between the AI agent and the Talonic API:

content.title: Architecture
content.code:
Agent (Claude Desktop / Cursor / Cline / etc.)
  ↓ MCP protocol over stdio
Talonic MCP server (this package)
  ↓ HTTPS, Bearer auth
api.talonic.com

content.text: Each tool call is one HTTP request to the Talonic API using your API key. The server handles auth, retries on transient failures (429, 5xx), MIME-type detection on file uploads, multipart serialisation, and structured error formatting.

title: Configuration
description: Environment variables for the Talonic MCP server: API key and optional base URL override.

content.text: Set via the `env` block in your MCP client config:

params.name: TALONIC_API_KEY
params.description: Your Talonic API key. Starts with `tlnc_`.

params.name: TALONIC_BASE_URL
params.description: Override the API base URL. Default: `https://api.talonic.com`.

title: Troubleshooting
description: Common issues and fixes for the Talonic MCP server: missing API key, invisible server, 500 errors, and filter problems.

content.text: TALONIC_API_KEY environment variable is required
The `env` block in your MCP client config is missing or not being read. Double-check the JSON shape. After editing, fully restart the client (not just the conversation).
Talonic does not appear in connected servers
Make sure the `command` is `npx` and `args` are exactly `["-y", "@talonic/mcp@latest"]`. Sanity check: run `npx -y @talonic/mcp@latest --version` in a terminal.
talonic_extract returns 500 with auto-discovery
Always provide either an inline `schema` or a `schema_id`. The auto-discovery code path is not yet stable.
unsupported_file_type error
The MIME type was inferred as `application/octet-stream`. Ensure the filename has a recognized extension (pdf, docx, png, jpg, etc.).
talonic_filter: No field matches name
Field names must be canonical names from the field registry (e.g. `vendor.name`). Call `talonic_search` first; canonical names appear in `fields[].canonicalName`.
Tool descriptions look wrong
Some MCP clients cache tool descriptions. Restart the client after a server update.
